column1: # NOTE: T6 sources are Thai MFA (mfa.go.th/en)
column3: and Khmer Times (khmertimeskh.com). Both MFA websites block direct WebFetch access (URL rejection / 403). Data below is sourced from Khmer Times (accessible) and MFA press releases recovered via search summaries. Direct MFA portal access requires manual browser review.

column5: Notes
Preah Vihear UNESCO listing July 2008 triggers bilateral crisis. Diplomatic protests exchanged. Ambassador-level relations maintained but strained. Joint boundary commission meetings suspended.

Nov 5: Thailand recalls ambassador after Hun Sen appoints Thaksin as economic advisor. Cambodia recalls ambassador the following day. Thailand revokes 2001 maritime MoU. Bilateral channels reduced to chargé d'affaires level. Lowest point since normalization.
Aug 2010: Thaksin resigns from Cambodia advisory role. Ambassadors reappointed to both capitals. JBC meetings resume. Channels repaired but trust fragile following 2009 rupture.

Feb 2011: armed clashes at Preah Vihear during ongoing diplomatic negotiations — clashes break out while Thai officials are in Cambodia. ICJ provisional measures issued. Diplomatic channels remain open but strained.
Yingluck government pursues normalization with Cambodia. JBC meetings resume. Bilateral trade channels reactivated. Diplomatic relations improving.
Relations continue to stabilize. Yingluck and Hun Sen maintain working relationship. No major diplomatic incidents. Regular bilateral mechanisms functioning.

May 2014 coup. Hun Sen welcomes Prayuth junta — ideological alignment between authoritarian governments. Bilateral channels reoriented under military-to-military framework. Trade and border cooperation continues.
Prayuth-Hun Sen bilateral relationship solid. Regular joint commission meetings. Scam economy compounds beginning to expand along border but not yet a formal diplomatic issue.
Cultural disputes emerge (Lakhon Khol vs khon UNESCO listing). Minor diplomatic friction but channels functioning. Bilateral trade growing.
CNRP dissolution concerns noted by Thailand but not escalated diplomatically. Bilateral channels intact. JBC meetings continue.
Post-sham-election Cambodia. Thailand does not formally protest. Channels maintained. Scam compound expansion continues without formal bilateral attention.
Both countries hold elections. Prayuth returned, Hun Manet preparation begins. Bilateral mechanisms functioning. 70th anniversary of diplomatic relations in planning.
Dec 19: PM exchange of congratulatory letters on 70th anniversary of diplomatic relations. COVID border management coordination. Channels cooperative despite border closures.
column6: Thai MFA / Cambodian MFA / Khmer Times

column5: Scam compound issue raised informally. Cambodian migrant worker abuses in Thailand cause friction. Formal channels maintained.
Scam economy pressure intensifies internationally. Thailand-Cambodia formal channels intact. Joint commission meetings ongoing.
Aug 2023: Hun Manet becomes PM. Srettha becomes Thai PM. New bilateral relationship being established. Strategic partnership elevation planned for early 2024.
column6: Thai MFA / Cambodian MFA / Khmer Times

column5: Feb 7: relations elevated to Strategic Partnership. 5 MoUs signed. $15B trade target set. Consulates-General announced in Siem Reap and Songkhla. Jun: Thai FM Maris visits Cambodia — positive bilateral meeting. Dec 17: Foreign Ministers phone call — channels active. Relationship at all-time high per Khmer Times.
column6: Thai MFA / Cambodian MFA / Khmer Times

column5: Feb 25: last positive FM bilateral meeting (ASEAN Future Forum sidelines). Apr: Paetongtarn visits Cambodia — last positive high-level contact. May 28: armed clashes. Jun 15: Paetongtarn-Hun Sen phone call leaked — bilateral channel severely damaged. Jul 23: Thailand downgrades relations. Jul 24: Cambodia reciprocates — both countries reduce to second chargé d'affaires level. All embassy staff recalled. Dec 27: China-brokered ceasefire + GBC joint statement — channels partially restored at working level.
column6: Thai MFA / Cambodian MFA / Khmer Times

column5: Ceasefire in place but contested. Cambodia urges Thailand to implement point 3 of Dec 27 joint declaration. Both sides at second chargé d'affaires level — ambassadors not yet reinstated. Anutin border wall and MoU 44 review undermine channel repair. Japan urges border reopening. International scrutiny ongoing.
